steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a contrast: more precise coordinates were calculated over decades, yet the prime meridian remained unchanged. The blank introduces that contrast — the transition word must signal "I acknowledge the new data, but surprisingly nothing changed."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Specifically,’: signals that what follows is a detail elaborating on the previous sentence. The new calculations aren't just a detail — they're the surprising part. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Again and again,’: stresses repetition. The passage isn't emphasizing how many times new coordinates were calculated; it's emphasizing that the meridian stayed put despite them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Granted,’: a concession word that sets up the structure "I acknowledge X, yet Y happened anyway." This frames the paradox precisely: granted that more precise coordinates emerged, the meridian still didn't move. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘To that end,’: signals purposeful follow-through, implying the second sentence was done in order to achieve the first sentence's goal. But the meridian not moving is the opposite of purposeful follow-through. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
